ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p (TA505 / GRACEFUL SPIDER) LEMURLOOT webshell deployment on MOVEit Transfer
ucs.description: Detects creation of the `human2.aspx` LEMURLOOT webshell — and/or the MOVEit IIS worker (w3wp.exe under the MOVEitTransfer AppPool) spawning a Windows shell — both of which are post-SQLi artefacts of the Cl0p MOVEit campaign (CVE-2023-34362) and the actor's reuse of the same tradecraft against GoAnywhere MFT, Cleo and PaperCut. The webshell filename and drop path are uniquely attributed to Cl0p/TA505 by Mandiant, Microsoft (Lace Tempest), and CISA AA23-158A.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_paths values(Filesystem.process_name) as writer_process from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="human2.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\MOVEitTransfer\\wwwroot\\*.ashx") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MOVEitTransfer*" OR Processes.parent_process="*MoveitDMZ*") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | eval signal=if(isnotnull(file_name),"LEMURLOOT_file_drop","MOVEit_webshell_child_proc") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p / TA505 LEMURLOOT (human2.aspx) on MOVEit Transfer + IIS-spawned shell
let Lookback = 7d;
union isfuzzy=true
(   DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
    | where FileName =~ "human2.aspx"
       or (FolderPath has_any (@"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot", @"\MoveitDMZ\wwwroot") and (FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx"))
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msiexec.exe","TrustedInstaller.exe","DismHost.exe")
    | extend Signal = "LEMURLOOT_FileDrop"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, FileName, FolderPath,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessAccountName, SHA256
),
(   DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
    | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MOVEitTransfer","MoveitDMZ")
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","powershell_ise.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","net.exe","whoami.exe")
    | extend Signal = "MOVEit_w3wp_shell_spawn"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
              InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath, SHA256
)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p / TA505 LEMURLOOT — MOVEit IIS shell spawn + human2.aspx drop on Sentinel
let Lookback = 7d;
union isfuzzy=true
(   SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith_cs "\\w3wp.exe"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("MOVEitTransfer","MoveitDMZ") or ParentProcessName has "MOVEit"
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("\\cmd.exe","\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe","\\powershell_ise.exe","\\cscript.exe","\\wscript.exe","\\bitsadmin.exe","\\certutil.exe","\\net.exe","\\whoami.exe")
    | extend Signal = "MOVEit_w3wp_shell_spawn"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Signal, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName
),
(   WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 11
    | extend TargetFilename = tostring(EventData.TargetFilename),
             Image          = tostring(EventData.Image)
    | where TargetFilename endswith "\\human2.aspx"
         or (TargetFilename has_any (@"\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\", @"\MoveitDMZ\wwwroot\") and (TargetFilename endswith ".aspx" or TargetFilename endswith ".ashx"))
    | where Image !endswith "\\msiexec.exe" and Image !endswith "\\TrustedInstaller.exe"
    | extend Signal = "LEMURLOOT_FileDrop"
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer, Signal, TargetFilename, Image
)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p / TA505 LEMURLOOT Webshell (human2.aspx) Deployment on MOVEit Transfer
id: e1f2a36b-7b1c-4a4d-9c84-7c0f1e0ca1c0
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects creation of the LEMURLOOT webshell (human2.aspx) inside the MOVEit Transfer or MoveitDMZ wwwroot directory.
  The filename and drop path are uniquely attributed to Cl0p / TA505 / GRACEFUL SPIDER (Microsoft: Lace Tempest)
  post-exploitation of the MOVEit Transfer SQL injection (CVE-2023-34362).
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-158a
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/zero-day-moveit-data-theft
  - https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/06/07/clop-ransomware-claims-moveit-attack/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0092/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g0092
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection_filename:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\human2.aspx'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\MOVEitTransfer\wwwroot\'
      - '\MoveitDMZ\wwwroot\'
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
  filter_install:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\TrustedInstaller.exe'
      - '\DismHost.exe'
  condition: selection_filename or (selection_path and not filter_install)
fields:
  - TargetFilename
  - Image
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate MOVEit Transfer product updates (validate against vendor MSI installer image and a maintenance window)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: The `human2.aspx` filename and its placement under the MOVEit Transfer wwwroot are signature artefacts of LEMURLOOT — a webshell that multiple vendors (Mandiant, Microsoft, CISA) attribute exclusively to Cl0p / TA505 / GRACEFUL SPIDER. Pairing the filesystem indicator with a second high-fidelity signal (w3wp.exe under the MOVEit AppPool spawning a Windows shell — extremely rare in legitimate MOVEi

ucs.title: [LLM] Cl0p ransomware encryption ritual — `.clop` / `.C_l_o_p` fan-out + `ClopReadMe.txt` / `README_README.txt` drops
ucs.description: Detects the simultaneous post-exploitation impact stage unique to Cl0p: a burst of file creations/renames carrying actor-specific extensions (`.clop`, `.C_l_o_p`, `.Cllp`, `.CIIp` — both lowercase L and capital-i obfuscation variants used by Cl0p builders) and/or the actor's signature ransom-note filenames (`README_README.txt`, `ClopReadMe.txt`, `Cl0pReadMe.txt`, `!_README_!.txt`) appearing across many folders on one host in a short window. Filenames and extension set are attributed exclusively to Cl0p by Unit42, McAfee ATR and Sophos.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count as file_events dc(Filesystem.file_path) as unique_files values(Filesystem.file_name) as sample_files values(Filesystem.process_name) as encryptor_process values(Filesystem.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where (Filesystem.file_name="*.clop" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.C_l_o_p" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.Cllp" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.CIIp" OR Filesystem.file_name="README_README.txt" OR Filesystem.file_name="ClopReadMe.txt" OR Filesystem.file_name="Cl0pReadMe.txt" OR Filesystem.file_name="!_README_!.txt") by Filesystem.dest _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | where file_events > 25 OR match(sample_files,"(?i)clopreadme|cl0preadme|README_README") | sort - _time
ucs.kql:
// Cl0p encryption fan-out: actor-specific extensions + ransom-note filenames
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileRenamed","FileModified")
| where FileName endswith ".clop"
     or FileName endswith ".C_l_o_p"
     or FileName endswith ".Cllp"
     or FileName endswith ".CIIp"
     or FileName =~ "README_README.txt"
     or FileName =~ "ClopReadMe.txt"
     or FileName =~ "Cl0pReadMe.txt"
     or FileName =~ "!_README_!.txt"
| summarize
    EncryptedFiles      = countif(FileName endswith ".clop" or FileName endswith ".C_l_o_p" or FileName endswith ".Cllp" or FileName endswith ".CIIp"),
    RansomNotes         = countif(FileName has_any ("README_README","ClopReadMe","Cl0pReadMe","!_README_!")),
    AffectedFolders     = dcount(FolderPath),
    Encryptors          = make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName, 16),
    EncryptorCmdLines   = make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, 8),
    EncryptorAccounts   = make_set(InitiatingProcessAccountName, 8),
    EncryptorSHA256     = make_set(InitiatingProcessSHA256, 8),
    FirstSeen           = min(Timestamp),
    LastSeen            = max(Timestamp)
    by DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 5m)
| where EncryptedFiles >= 25 or RansomNotes >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cl0p encryption fan-out on Sentinel via Sysmon FileCreate (EID 11)
WindowsEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon" and EventID == 11
| extend TargetFilename = tostring(EventData.TargetFilename),
         Image          = tostring(EventData.Image),
         User           = tostring(EventData.User)
| where TargetFilename endswith ".clop"
     or TargetFilename endswith ".C_l_o_p"
     or TargetFilename endswith ".Cllp"
     or TargetFilename endswith ".CIIp"
     or TargetFilename endswith "\\README_README.txt"
     or TargetFilename endswith "\\ClopReadMe.txt"
     or TargetFilename endswith "\\Cl0pReadMe.txt"
     or TargetFilename endswith "\\!_README_!.txt"
| summarize
    EncryptedFiles  = countif(TargetFilename endswith ".clop" or TargetFilename endswith ".C_l_o_p" or TargetFilename endswith ".Cllp" or TargetFilename endswith ".CIIp"),
    RansomNotes     = countif(TargetFilename has_any ("README_README","ClopReadMe","Cl0pReadMe","!_README_!")),
    Encryptors      = make_set(Image, 8),
    Users           = make_set(User, 8),
    FirstSeen       = min(TimeGenerated),
    LastSeen        = max(TimeGenerated)
    by Computer, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where EncryptedFiles >= 25 or RansomNotes >= 1
| order by FirstSeen desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cl0p Ransomware Impact — Actor Extension or Ransom Note File Create
id: 3a8d4d1d-5b6e-4c69-9a73-7c4f8b4b4f02
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects file create/rename events that produce Cl0p-specific extensions (.clop, .C_l_o_p, .Cllp, .CIIp)
  or Cl0p-specific ransom-note filenames (README_README.txt, ClopReadMe.txt, Cl0pReadMe.txt, !_README_!.txt).
  Both indicator sets are uniquely attributed to Cl0p / TA505 by Unit42, McAfee ATR and Sophos. A single
  hit on these names is high-fidelity; the matching SPL/KQL queries additionally require a fan-out
  threshold to suppress single-file analyst samples.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/clop-ransomware/
  - https://www.sophos.com/en-us/news/2023/06/05/clop-at-the-top
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0092/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.g0092
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: file_event
detection:
  ext_selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.clop'
      - '.C_l_o_p'
      - '.Cllp'
      - '.CIIp'
  note_selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\README_README.txt'
      - '\ClopReadMe.txt'
      - '\Cl0pReadMe.txt'
      - '\!_README_!.txt'
  condition: ext_selection or note_selection
fields:
  - TargetFilename
  - Image
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Malware-analysis labs handling Cl0p samples on instrumented hosts
  - Backup/restore of historic Cl0p-encrypted files into a forensics share
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Both indicator sets are signature-unique to Cl0p: the `.clop` / `.C_l_o_p` / `.Cllp` / `.CIIp` extension family (note the typo-squatted variants `Cllp` and `CIIp` — lower-L vs capital-i — Cl0p builders intentionally rotate these across affiliates) and the actor's distinctive ransom-note filenames (`ClopReadMe.txt`, `Cl0pReadMe.txt`, `README_README.txt`, `!_README_!.txt`) are documented across Unit
